blocks.content: Chapter 108: Return to Daily Life (3)
Siena smiled and glanced sideways at Etella.
She had been tormented by overwhelming guilt for the past ten days.
As expected, Etella approached Shirone with a face that looked ready to burst into tears at any moment.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Teacher, I'm sorry. I've done it again….
Without even waiting to hear Shirone's words, Etella pulled him into a tight embrace.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: I'm sorry. It's my fault. If only I'd been more careful. This won't happen again.
To their friends, it looked like a beautiful embrace between teacher and student, but for Shirone, who was on the receiving end, it was pure torture.
Etella hugged him so tightly that he couldn't breathe.
Embarrassment aside, he genuinely felt like he was about to suffocate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Uh, um, Teacher….

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Hmm?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry, but… please let me live.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Oh my!
When Shirone's voice came out muffled, Etella hurriedly released him.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I was so happy I couldn't help myself….
Shirone gasped for air, his face pale.
But even this sensation was part of the present world.
Smiling at the sheer joy of being back, he said:

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now it really feels like I've returned.
When their friends' eyes began to gleam mischievously, Shirone realized his mistake and frantically waved his hands.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, no, it's not like that. I mean, this is….

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You little brat, what's all this 'this and that'?
Amy rushed over and pinched Shirone's cheek.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What? Do I not feel real enough to you? Huh?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No! That's not what I meant!
Nade and Iruki burst into laughter.
Alpheas watched Shirone interacting with his friends with a fond expression before quietly slipping out of the infirmary.
From now on, he would be the busiest person.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Headmaster.
Turning around, he saw Shirone, who had somehow slipped out unnoticed, closing the infirmary door and walking toward him.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: What is it? You should rest for a while. Catch up with your friends. The school will likely remain closed for some time anyway.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, thank you for your concern. But… there's something I've been curious about.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Hmm? What's that?
Shirone recounted everything that had happened—from the moment he entered the infinite realm to his return to the original world through the black hole.
At first, Alpheas listened with interest. But as the story progressed, his expression gradually hardened.
Especially when Shirone described the woman's abilities, he closed his eyes and sighed.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Did that woman say she knew me?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. She asked me to send her regards to you. My memory is hazy, but I think that's what she said.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone wondered if he had brought up something unnecessary.
Alpheas's expression was far worse than he had anticipated.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: I see. I think I know who she is.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? You know her?
Shirone's eyes widened.
Alpheas was undoubtedly a great mage, but the woman existed in a realm beyond life itself. It was strange that he had met both of them, given they resided in different worlds.
But Alpheas's thoughts were different.
It wasn't a coincidence but inevitability.
Shirone meeting her was predetermined from the moment he awakened the Immortal Function.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who is she, really?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Adrias Miro. In her time, she was called the Labyrinth of Space-Time. She was my disciple, specializing in Scale Magic.
Shirone's expression turned blank.
He had never imagined she was Alpheas's disciple.
Learning that her abilities, which he had thought were divine, were actually magic came as a shock.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is Scale Magic?
Alpheas stroked his beard as he pondered how to explain.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Shirone, in the magical society, those who have attained the Immortal Function are called Unlockers.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I've heard that.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Then this will be easier. Miro is also an Unlocker. Even as a child, her curiosity was extraordinary. She delved endlessly—into worlds larger than the universe and smaller than particles. Scale Magic is a form of magic only those who have comprehended the infinity of space-time can wield.
Shirone recalled his encounter with Miro.
Indeed, her primary ability, the glass orb, freely manipulated the relativity of space-time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Miro said she had been watching me for a long time. Is that possible?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Don't worry. Miro exists in a different dimension from you. But when you unlocked the Immortal Function, you momentarily reached the same dimension. She must have been waiting for that moment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But why me? Unlockers are rare, but according to Arcane, they're not that few in number. There must be many Unlockers stronger than me.
Alpheas hesitated, stroking his beard.
What he was about to say was a top-secret matter known only to teachers who had served for over 20 years.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Miro was the founding president of the Supernatural Psychic Science Research Club.
Shirone pictured Miro's face.
It was surprising enough that she had founded the club he now belonged to, but as the first president, she would have been active nearly 20 years ago, yet she seemed barely aged.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Miro wasn't fixated on phenomena. She believed there were too many things knowledge couldn't explain. Together with her peers, she established the research club in Istas. Even Micaea Gaold, the current head of the Thorhmia Magic Association, was one of the early members.
Shirone imagined the members of that time.
According to Nade, the master key of Istas's magic vault was created by a servant ability user similar to Iruki.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It couldn't have been Miro or Gaold.
He could roughly guess how extraordinary the early members must have been.
As Shirone lost himself in thought, Alpheas also reminisced about events from 18 years ago.
Miro had been a good kid.
If not for that day's incident…
Suddenly, he realized he had omitted something and turned back to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Shirone, do you know how many students there are in total?
It didn't seem like he expected an answer, as Alpheas continued immediately.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Of course, the total number changes. Some graduate, take leaves of absence, or face disciplinary action. But no matter the number, this school is always short by one person.
Alpheas turned his gaze out the window as he spoke.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Miro never graduated. She still resides in this school.
Shirone gulped.
It felt like hearing a ghost story passed down through the school.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But what did it mean for her to “reside” here?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hadn't he just said she lived in another dimension?
At that moment, Scale Magic flashed through Shirone's mind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then… could it be…?
Alpheas nodded.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Yes. Miro is in Istas. Of course, it's a higher dimension created by Scale Magic. Those who know call it the Upper Layer.
Alpheas placed a hand on Shirone's shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Shirone, promise me this. Don't speak of today's events to anyone. The Upper Layer is nothing you should meddle with. Never, ever involve yourself with it. It could plunge the world into chaos.
Shirone couldn't answer immediately.
An unknown world. Unlockers. Scale Magic. All this information was shaking his mind.
Of course, he had no intention of recklessly stepping into danger.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But promising never to get involved would be a lie in some way.
Alpheas sighed as if he had expected this.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: If you truly wish to learn about Miro, I'll allow you to investigate the Kergo Ruins. I'm not suggesting this to placate you. It's far too dangerous to dive straight into the Upper Layer. So please, at least abide by this.
Finally, Shirone nodded.
Alpheas wouldn't be so strict without good reason.
And Shirone himself also preferred a gradual approach.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I understand. But knowing she's in Istas is eerie. I don't think I'll be able to sleep in the research club. What if she suddenly appears?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Don't worry. Miro would never leave the space she created.
Shirone tilted his head.
Such certainty implied there was proof.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But she's an extraordinary person. Is there a special reason?
Alpheas looked out the window with a sorrowful expression.
His words weren't spoken with absolute confidence.
But he knew Miro would never return.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: For Miro… this world is far too painful.
Alpheas, Canis, and Arin left for the capital, Bashka, to undergo an investigation by the Magic Association.
According to the teachers, the school would remain temporarily closed until the Association's decision was finalized.
Shirone was admitted to the infirmary.
The medical staff decided to monitor his condition since his heart had stopped for ten days.
Shirone, too, had no desire to do anything for the time being, so he passed the time with his friends.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Shirone, I'm sorry. You must have felt so lonely.
Seriel spoke with a tearful expression.
The thing she regretted most after regaining her memories was pushing Shirone away harshly when he brought her coffee.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's fine. It was because of the magic. You didn't do anything wrong, Seriel-senpai.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Even so, how could I forget you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Really, it's okay. Please don't dwell on it.
Seriel's eyes softened like a deer's.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Shirone, you're so kind. So, what will you do now? Will you demand my body?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh?
Shirone's face twisted in disbelief.
His friends' reactions were no different.
Amy shook her head vigorously, pressing her fingers to her forehead.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Seriel, what kind of books have you been reading lately?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: This one!
As if she had been waiting for the question, Seriel pulled out a novel.
The cover was provocatively red, with the title "Primitive Noose" written on it.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: This is the hottest book in bookstores right now. It's about a married nobleman who falls in love with a maid. There's this line: 'I've broken your plate. What will you do now? Will you demand my body?' And then the nobleman ties her up tightly with a rope and….

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Stop! That's enough!
Amy raised her hand to cut her off.
It felt like they shouldn't hear any more.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: What's wrong? The interesting part is just starting.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Seriel, no matter how much you love novels, don't confuse them with reality. Those books use sensationalism to captivate readers.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Hmph! You're just saying that because you haven't read it. If you gave it a try, you'd be hooked. Want to borrow it?
As Seriel held out "Primitive Noose," Amy quickly turned her head away.
If she accepted the offer now, she'd be subjected to at least a week of Seriel's enthusiastic reviews.
Shirone leaned back against a cushion and sighed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah… Today's the last day in the infirmary. Thanks for visiting me all this time. You too, Seriel-senpai.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Why the sad talk? Honestly, I was dying from all the studying in the graduating class. Amy and I were glad for the break.
Amy didn't deny it.
The daily competition for rankings had pushed her stress to the limit.
The temporary school closure was a blessing for graduating students.
Seriel's expression turned gloomy.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: But I'm worried about the headmaster. Honestly, this decision was unexpected. It's something the Creas autonomous region could handle, but he insisted on undergoing the Magic Association's inspection.
Alpheas had voluntarily submitted to the indictment.
Given his high reputation on the continent, he could have easily smoothed things over, but he fully accepted the student council's claims.
As a result, the case was transferred to Inspection Division 3, known as the most ruthless in the Magic Association.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Inspection Division 3 is called the special investigation unit of the Magic Association. They follow the law strictly, ignoring connections or favors. Even Headmaster Alpheas won't escape disciplinary action.
Shirone thought it was for the best.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The headmaster has carried his pain for 40 years. If this opportunity can lighten his burden even slightly, I think accepting punishment is a good thing.
Nade chimed in.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Let's wait and see. Who are we even worrying about? Alpheas, a certified 4th-class mage. Once the school reopens, we'll be busy again, so let's rest while we can.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah. That sounds good.
Shirone looked out the window.
The afternoon sun cast a drowsy light.
Capital, Bashka.
Viewed from the Zije Mountains, Bashka's landscape was dominated by the royal palace towering into the sky, with key institutions spreading concentrically around it.
The capital's residents said that the buildings visible from the Zije Mountains symbolized the nation.
One of them was the Magic Association.
The 18-story building managed all matters concerning mages registered in the Thorhmia Kingdom and handled magical society's oversight and intelligence tasks in coordination with other institutions.
Any mage would want to visit at least once, but Sad, assigned as the escort for Alpheas, Canis, and Arin, felt like a cow being led to slaughter.

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: Master, is this really the best course?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: It's what I wanted. My only regret is that it took so long.
Alpheas's resolve was firm.
Sad hadn't asked with much expectation.
But once they crossed the Association's threshold, there would be no turning back, so he wanted to confirm his master's will.

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: Understood. Then I'll wait until the trial concludes.
Alpheas wore a cheerful expression.
With Canis and Arin in tow, he turned himself in to the Inspection Division, and the three were each confined to solitary cells to await their fate.
The next day, Alpheas was the first to enter the interrogation room.
Meanwhile, Canis and Arin waited in an isolation facility lined with magical devices.
The mana suppression devices made entering the Spirit Zone impossible.
But beyond that, even Harvest couldn't function.
After about three hours, the summons came.
Canis, Arin. Come out.
Though not officially convicted yet, the Magic Association staff already treated the two like criminals.
Likely because they had heard Alpheas's testimony.
Canis and Arin silently complied.
As they entered the hallway leading to the interrogation room, they saw Alpheas walking toward them from the opposite end.
Canis planned to pass by indifferently, but just before they crossed paths, Alpheas turned to the guard.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Sorry, could you give us a moment to talk?

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Ah, yes. But not too long.
Canis snorted at the guard's deferential attitude, so different from how he treated them.
Alpheas received respect not for his strength but his fame.
It irked him to see the man exuding the victor's composure despite being weaker than Arcane.
Once the guards stepped away, Alpheas spoke.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Don't be too nervous. Just answer a few questions honestly.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: You're mistaken. We're not the docile students you teach. We've fought countless battles. No situation frightens us.
Canis, having experienced life-and-death battles, possessed something his peers lacked.
But such a life had also cost him much.
Alpheas wanted to help him and Arin reclaim what they had lost.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Actually, that's why I wanted to see you.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What do you mean?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Once this is over, how about enrolling in the magic school?
Canis felt like he'd been insulted.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Did this man not understand how he had lived?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: After learning magic through life-and-death struggles, was he supposed to mix with hothouse flowers?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Don't mock me. My master didn't lose because he was weak. If not for Abyss Nova—if we had fought properly—I could have blown you away in one hit.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: That might have been possible.
Alpheas readily admitted it.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: But if it hadn't been that way, I might have knelt first and apologized.
Canis bit his lip.
The words were unacceptable, but he couldn't refute them.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Canis, evil is like that. No matter how justified your reasons, you'll still be hated. You can rise higher. I want to offer you a bright future, not darkness.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Why do you care about me? Is this pity?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: I was also Arcane's disciple. That makes me your senior. Just as time can't be reversed, connections last forever. If you want to start a new life, I'll help.
Canis didn't respond.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What kind of new life could there be now?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: All he knew was battle, and thus, his fate was to live on the battlefield.
As Alpheas walked away, the guards seized Canis and Arin and dragged them to the interrogation room.
Unlike the infernal prison they had imagined, the room was neat and tidy.
A diminutive man sat at the table with his back turned.
From behind, all they could see was his silver hair.
Inspector, these are the suspects in this case, Canis and Arin.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Ah, come this way.
His voice was like grating metal.
True to his reputation as a field agent of the infamous Inspection Division 3, his aura was razor-sharp.
Canis and Arin circled the table to face the inspector.
Contrary to expectations, his appearance wasn't intimidating.
He had a cat-like pointed chin and narrow eyes that smiled.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: I'm Inspector Sakiri of Inspection Division 3. You are Canis and Arin, correct?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Yes.
Canis answered calmly.
But no response came from Arin.
Not wanting to provoke the inspector, Canis turned to her.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin, what's wrong?
Arin trembled, her face pale.
Canis deduced from her shaken eyes that she had seen something shocking.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What could make her react like this?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Canis, I can't read him….
It was impossible for Arin, an Awakened, to fail to perceive emotions unless there were none to begin with or they had been artificially erased.
To Arin, Sakiri's form was grotesque.
His entire body was smooth like mirrored metal, and his egg-shaped face lacked any features.
His hands and feet were conical, making even the slightest emotion impossible to discern.
It was the form of an Awakened who had perfectly suppressed all emotion.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Oh dear, the lady seems startled. It's called Awakening, right? We can block magic, but innate traits are harder to suppress. Not that I expect you two to cause trouble.
Sakiri, perhaps intrigued, set down his pen and crossed his legs.
Arin watched as his conical legs twisted.
They coiled like pretzels, then unraveled repeatedly.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: This man is toying with us.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: He wasn't just suppressing emotions.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: He could control them perfectly.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Focus.
Arin jerked her head up.
The inspector's mirror-smooth face elongated into a cone, stopping just before her forehead.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Let's proceed. We're on official business, after all. Arin, correct?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Y-yes.
Terrified, Arin wasn't even sure if she had answered.
The sharp cone aimed at her forehead was likely Sakiri's piercing gaze.
She had never seen an Awakened form so pristine.
It made her think no criminal could escape this man unscathed.
Canis and Arin finally faced reality.
They were in the clutches of the kingdom's most powerful and authoritative judicial body.
As the two sat deflated, Sakiri's needle-like face returned to its smooth, metallic egg shape.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Well, we've prepared a statement based on Alpheas's testimony, but there are a few things to confirm. Answer my questions.
Sakiri checked various points through Q&A.
Arin and Canis answered honestly.
After about an hour, Sakiri tidied the documents with a satisfied expression.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Yes. Most of the statements align. There are discrepancies, but nothing major.
His words were oddly casual for an inspector.
But Sakiri continued as if it were trivial.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Isn't this a form of brainwashing? Arcane exploited your unhappy lives to brainwash you from childhood.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What?
Canis's eyes sharpened.
He'd rather be called a villain.
Sakiri's words negated the very foundation of his life.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Master never forced anything. I acted on my own. Everything was my doing.
Arin, aware of the inspector's strength, grew anxious as Canis flared up.
But Sakiri's Awakened form remained unchanged.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: A vicious method. He saved you, made you revere him, then said things like, 'I need this, but it's not like you have to do it.'
Canis didn't understand.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: He was here to atone.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Now, suddenly, it was brainwashing?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Were they planning to send him to a mental hospital?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: So what do you want me to do? What changes if I admit to being brainwashed?

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Well… you'd both be acquitted.
Canis's expression went blank.
He had followed Arcane to break criminals out of Inferno.
He had endangered hundreds of lives at the magic school.
Even if he wasn't the mastermind, acquittal was impossible.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Acquitted…? Us?

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Ah, of course, only if you admit to being brainwashed. The Association is focusing on that possibility.
Admit it, and they'd be free.
Both of them could start new lives as if nothing had happened.
For once, Canis felt his blood run cold.
What was stranger was the Association's stance.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: In a time when catching criminals was paramount, why were they so eager to let them go?

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Ah, one more thing.
Sakiri rummaged through the documents as if he had just remembered.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Maybe he had.
Arin couldn't read his emotions, after all.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Arcane's lawyer sent a formal notice. He requested anonymity, but it's been notarized. Take a look. His asset records, will, and inheritance documents.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Will?
Sakiri slid the documents across the table as if no explanation was needed.
Canis checked them with trembling hands.
Arcane's will was unremarkable, listing matters to be handled posthumously in a calm tone.
Among them was this line:
Chapter Document: All assets transferred to Canis.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Master….
He didn't think of Arcane as secretly warm or caring.
The man had been unapologetically firm in his beliefs, so his treatment of Canis had likely been equally honest.
Arcane proved it with his will.
Though he had seemed indifferent to his disciple's life, he had considered Canis his only student.
Setting aside the will, Canis checked the asset list.
Real estate, including dungeons, magical equipment, expensive artifacts, rare herbs, and bank deposits were neatly itemized.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: 1, 10, 100, thousand, ten thousand….
Canis counted the total at the end.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: 4.8 billion gold….
An amount befitting the wealth accumulated by the great mage Arcane over 150 years.
Yet Canis couldn't comprehend the current situation.
First, they were suddenly acquitted, and now he was the heir to a 4.8-billion-gold fortune.
Sakiri scratched his head awkwardly.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: But there's a slight problem. Arcane left his assets to you, clearly acknowledging you as his disciple. But the Association sees brainwashing as implanting a false self. In the end, you were deceived by Arcane. Otherwise, how could you commit such atrocities? That's why the Association is considering acquittal. But if that happens….

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I can't inherit? Because I'm no longer his disciple?
Sakiri smiled warmly for the first time.
Of course, his Awakened form didn't change a whit.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Yes. Exactly. Arcane's heir is the brainwashed Canis, not the un-brainwashed one. The money would be seized as illegal funds and returned to the Association. So before that, we wanted your thoughts. Admit to being brainwashed, and you'll be acquitted. Just sign here.
Sakiri slid another document forward.
Canis stared at it blankly.
A waiver of inheritance.
Admitting to brainwashing would acquit them.
They were merely Arcane's victims.
But the fortune would end up in someone else's hands.
In the end, the Association had chosen 4.8 billion gold over two insignificant kids.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: I can give you time, but it might hurt your case. It could imply voluntary crime. Honestly, does it make sense? Of course you were brainwashed. Why else would you commit such heinous acts?
Canis's mind went blank.
He wasn't afraid to fight and die.
But the inspector said they were innocent.
He had clearly done wrong, yet it was as if he hadn't.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: This place is no different from Radum.
The outside world wasn't a hothouse bathed in sunlight either.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It was a battlefield where colossal desires swirled under the guise of law.
As Canis hesitated, Sakiri clicked his tongue and stood.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Then I'll give you some time. About an hour—

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I'll sign.
Sakiri, halfway up, paused.
Then he smiled and sat back down.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: A wise choice. Here….
He handed over his own fountain pen.
Canis signed the waiver.
The pen scratched against the paper.
Sakiri took it back with a pleased smile and checked the document.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Congratulations on your freedom. Now that it's over, let me say you made the right choice. Hiring a lawyer would have complicated things.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: A lawyer.
Canis hadn't even considered it.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Maybe he should have.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: No, Sakiri wasn't lying.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: If the Magic Association had been lenient, they wouldn't have presented the waiver in the first place.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What do we do now?

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Hmm. Well, you can leave. You're free.
Canis and Arin had been thoroughly outplayed.
But Sakiri, as if it were none of his concern, gathered the documents and turned away.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Where do we go for the release procedures….

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: There aren't any. Just leave.
The dismissive attitude made Canis realize the truth.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: From the start, the Magic Association hadn't cared about them.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: They had only spared two hours to reclaim Arcane's wealth.
It wasn't just his pride that was wounded.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: The Association's overwhelming power, which he could do nothing about, was far more humiliating. It made him realize just how extraordinary Arcane had been to stand against nations.
Canis regained his composure.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It was clear now.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: He was a battle mage.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: And knowing how to fight, he had no more hesitation.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Hey, you.
Sakiri turned at the door.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Yes?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: You're a mage, right?

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Hmm, you could say that. All Association staff are mages.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What's your rank?

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Certified 5th class, why?
Canis gauged his opponent's level.
An inspector of the Magic Association was a 5th-class mage.
That was the kind of man he was.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: This isn't the end for me. I'll never stay down.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: I see. As you should. Well, I'll be going.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Someday… I'll make you my subordinate.
Sakiri's hand on the doorknob froze.
After a moment's thought, he turned back with a thin smile.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: You'll have to study hard. This position is highly competitive. But if by some chance that day comes….
Sakiri placed his right hand over his heart.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: I'll be in your care.
Canis didn't react to the inspector's uncharacteristic gesture.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: He only realized how thoroughly pragmatic Sakiri was.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Bowing cost nothing.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: The future was anyone's guess.
As Sakiri left, Arin smiled at Canis.
At least they were free.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Wasn't that better than rotting in prison with 4.8 billion gold?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Canis, you made the right choice.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: There are… so many strong people.
Canis's lips curled faintly.
With a new goal, his heart pounded as fiercely as when he had first met Arcane.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin, let's get stronger.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Yeah….
Arin looked at the door Sakiri had exited.
Canis would return here someday.
Since this wasn't their end, she too could move forward.
Leaving the interrogation room, Sakiri headed straight for the Inspector's Office.
The matter had been resolved easily given its gravity.
Arcane's death had been pivotal.
There were no legal conflicts, and Alpheas's admission of guilt had helped.
The Inspector's Office was an elegant room with marble floors.
A man known as the Iron Inspector, a legend of Inspection Division 3, stood with his hands behind his back, gazing out the window.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Inspector, the approval documents for this case.

blocks.speaker: Iron Inspector
blocks.content: Leave them on the desk.
Sakiri placed the file on the desk.
Normally, he would leave, but this time curiosity got the better of him, and he turned at the door.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Is this really alright for the Alpheas case?

blocks.speaker: Iron Inspector
blocks.content: Why? Do you think there'll be complaints from above?

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: A six-month suspension is lenient. But I doubt there'll be complaints. It's a 40-year-old case with no intent to harm, and converting 4.8 billion gold into bail seems fair.

blocks.speaker: Iron Inspector
blocks.content: Then why ask?
The question implied things were proceeding as expected.
Sakiri regretted stirring trouble but couldn't backtrack now.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: I wondered if there was an element of atonement.
The inspector was silent for a long time.
Then he slowly turned to face Sakiri.

blocks.speaker: Iron Inspector
blocks.content: I did feel guilty toward Alpheas. But I've never let personal feelings dictate a criminal's punishment.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: My apologies. That was out of line.

blocks.speaker: Iron Inspector
blocks.content: You may leave.
Flustered, Sakiri hurried out.
Though infamous among criminals, within the Association, he was just another mage wary of his superior.
The inspector opened the file.
After carefully reviewing the contents, he took out a fountain pen and brought it to the approval line.
Countless memories flashed through his mind.
Now a white-haired old man, recalling those days made his jaw ache where Alpheas had struck him.

blocks.speaker: Iron Inspector
blocks.content: Tch. I should've brought a gift.
After the incident with Erina, he had never visited Alpheas again, but he had lived refusing to accept his defeat.
Yet over 40 years, photon theory had evolved and revolutionized, continuing to change the world even now.

blocks.speaker: Iron Inspector
blocks.content: He could no longer deny how genius Alpheas's achievements had been.

blocks.speaker: Iron Inspector
blocks.content: Heh. A belated gift. The Gold Circle was rightfully yours.
The inspector inscribed his signature on the document.
Ardiano Sarov.
